title: Lepanthopsis farrago
intro: Lepanthopsis farrago, commonly known as Farrago's Lepanthes or False Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae) that is native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throat, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lepanthopsis farrago include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in tinctures and other herbal preparations to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Lepanthopsis farrago is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Lepanthopsis is a subgenus of the genus Lepanthes, which contains a wide range of small to medium-sized orchids. Lepanthopsis farrago is native to Central America and is found in the tropical regions of the continent. The species is known to thrive in humid environments with rich soil and filtered sunlight. Lepanthopsis farrago is typically characterized by its small, delicate flowers and long, slender stems.
